title: eremophila occidens
intro: Eremophila occidens, also known as the western emu bush, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, tubular flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Eremophila occidens include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing protection against oxidative stress. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
